content.text: Do 7 and only seven of the following.

problem.text:
Let \(X \subset \mathbb{R}\) consist of all numbers of the form
\[
1+\frac{1}{\sqrt{p}}+\cdots+\frac{1}{\sqrt{p^n}}
\]
with \(p\) prime and \(n \in \mathbb{Z}_+\). Show that \(X\) is countable.
Assume \(f:X\to Y\) is a continuous bijection with \(X\) compact and \(Y\) Hausdorff. Show that \(f\) is a homeomorphism.
Prove that a compact Hausdorff space is normal.
Assume that \(f:X\to Y\) is continuous and surjective.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: If \(X\) is Lindelöf, show that \(Y\) is also.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: If \(X\) is separable, show that \(Y\) is also.

problem.text: Each \(A_\lambda \subset X\) is connected for \(\lambda \in \Lambda\) and \(\bigcap_{\lambda\in\Lambda} A_\lambda \ne \varnothing\), then prove that \(\bigcup_{\lambda\in\Lambda} A_\lambda\) is connected.
Show that a subspace of a complete metric space is itself complete if and only if it is a closed subspace.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define the metric \(\rho\) on \([-1,1]^{\mathbb{Z}_+}\) that gives it the uniform topology.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Show that in this metric the unit ball, \(B_1(\underline{0})\), is not limit point compact where \(\underline{0}\) is the sequence \(0,0,0,\ldots\).

problem.text: Prove or disprove: The real line \(\mathbb{R}_\ell\) with the lower limit topology is connected.
